page: Vultr vx1-m-2c-16g-120s is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features an x86_64 AMD EPYC processor with 2 vCPUs mapped to 1 physical core via hyperthreading, operating at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The instance is configured with a shared CPU allocation model and includes 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is provided by a bundled 120 GB NVMe SSD, and the server includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks reveal a distinct contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core execution is strong, as evidenced by a stress-ng single-core CPU score of 3040.08 and high-tier single-threaded bzip3 decompression at 14.79 GB/sec. However, multi-core performance is limited by the shared CPU architecture and single physical core, with stress-ng multi-core and Passmark CPU scores falling into the lower 25% tier. Memory performance is average, with large-block read bandwidth at 44.5 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 39.83 GB/sec.

From a resource density perspective, this server offers a cost-efficient profile for memory-intensive workloads that do not require sustained, dedicated multi-core CPU performance. The combination of 16.0 GB of RAM and 120 GB of NVMe SSD storage on a shared CPU model makes it suitable for caching layers, lightweight database operations, static web serving, and single-threaded data processing tasks.
description: Vultr vx1-m-2c-16g-120s is a memory-optimized virtual server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring 2 vCPUs on a shared AMD EPYC processor, 16.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 120 GB NVMe SSD. Operating on an x86_64 architecture, the server delivers strong single-core performance, highlighted by top-tier single-threaded bzip3 decompression and strong single-core stress-ng results. However, its shared CPU allocation and single physical core limit multi-core scaling, resulting in lower-tier multi-core and database benchmark scores. Memory bandwidth remains average for large blocks. This hardware configuration is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy, low-CPU-utilization workloads, making it suitable for static web serving, caching, and lightweight single-threaded processing.
og_description: Vultr vx1-m-2c-16g-120s is a memory-optimized virtual server featuring 2 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and 120 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong single-core performance for lightweight workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-m-2c-16g-120s is a memory-optimized virtual server with 2 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16.0 GB RAM, and 120 GB NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server with shared AMD EPYC processors and NVMe storage, optimized for single-threaded tasks and caching.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC processor with 2 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM
Bundled 120 GB NVMe SSD local storage
Strong single-core performance and single-threaded decompression
Shared CPU allocation model with average large-block memory bandwidth
Best suited for caching, static web serving, and lightweight databases
